description: Multi-type bank transaction processor with tier-based limits, overdraft protection, transfer fees, fraud detection, and dormant account handling. Based on IBM Enterprise COBOL sample patterns.

explicit.description: Transaction type must be DEP, WDR, TRF, or BAL
explicit.effect: REJECT with E001 if type invalid

explicit.description: Source account must exist in account master
explicit.effect: REJECT with E002 if account not found

explicit.description: Closed accounts cannot perform any transactions
Frozen accounts allow only balance inquiries
explicit.effect: REJECT with E004 if status = 'F' and type != 'BAL'

explicit.description: Transaction amount must be positive for non-inquiry transactions
explicit.effect: REJECT with E006 if amount <= 0 and type != 'BAL'

explicit.description: Withdrawal amount limited by account tier: Standard $5000, Premium $25000, VIP $100000
explicit.effect: REJECT with E007/E008/E009 if amount exceeds tier limit

explicit.description: Target account must exist for transfers
explicit.effect: REJECT with E014 if target account not found

explicit.description: Target account must not be closed for transfers
explicit.effect: REJECT with E015 if target status = 'X'

implicit.description: Dormant accounts are reactivated by deposits but reject all other transaction types
implicit.effect: If status = 'D' and type = 'DEP', change status to 'A'; else REJECT with E005
implicit.implicit_reason: Reactivation is a side effect buried in the dormant check — no explicit 'reactivate' logic

implicit.description: Daily withdrawal count limited by tier: Standard 3, Premium 10, VIP unlimited
implicit.effect: REJECT with E010 if count >= tier limit
implicit.implicit_reason: VIP accounts silently skip the count check entirely via CONTINUE — there is no explicit 'unlimited' rule

implicit.description: Overdraft protection: checking accounts can go negative up to overdraft_limit with $35 fee; savings/business accounts have no overdraft
implicit.effect: If checking and abs(new_balance) <= overdraft_limit: allow and charge $35 fee. If non-checking: REJECT with E012. If checking but exceeds limit: REJECT with E011
implicit.implicit_reason: Overdraft fee is applied as a subtraction from the already-negative balance, and the account-type branching determines which error code is used

implicit.description: Savings accounts must maintain minimum balance of $100 after withdrawal
implicit.effect: REJECT with E013 if savings account and new_balance < 100.00
implicit.implicit_reason: This check happens AFTER overdraft logic, so it only applies when the balance is positive but below threshold

implicit.description: Fraud flag raised for withdrawals and transfers exceeding $10,000
implicit.effect: Set fraud_flag = 'Y' if (WDR or TRF) and amount > 10000. Result code changes to W001/W002 instead of 0000
implicit.implicit_reason: The transaction still succeeds — the fraud flag is a silent side effect that changes only the result code

implicit.description: Transfer fee: Standard tier pays $25 fee, Premium and VIP pay no fee. Fee is deducted from source account in addition to transfer amount
implicit.effect: effective_debit = amount + fee. REJECT with E016 if source balance < effective_debit
implicit.implicit_reason: The fee calculation is separate from the transfer logic and the effective amount check combines both — not obvious from the fee constants alone

implicit.description: Balance inquiry succeeds for frozen accounts (exception to freeze rule)
implicit.effect: If status = 'F' and type = 'BAL', return current balance without error
implicit.implicit_reason: The freeze check has an embedded NOT condition that exempts BAL — this exception is easy to miss

data_constraints.description: Withdrawal subtracts amount (and possibly overdraft fee) from balance
Transfer debits source by (amount + fee) and credits target by amount
data_constraints.formula: source_new = source_old - amount - fee; target_new = target_old + amount

data_constraints.description: Daily withdrawal counter incremented on each successful withdrawal

test_scenarios.description: Happy path: deposit into active checking account
Withdrawal from active Standard account within limits

test_scenarios.expected_result: SUCCESS with status changed to A

test_scenarios.description: Overdraft protection for checking account within limit
Savings account rejects withdrawal below $100 minimum
Large withdrawal triggers fraud flag but succeeds
test_scenarios.expected_result: SUCCESS with fraud_flag = Y, result_code = W001

test_scenarios.description: Transfer with Standard tier fee ($25) deducted from source
Transfer to closed target account rejected
Transfer fails when source balance < amount + fee
